exchange_a.name: Bybit
strengths: Strong derivatives focus with unified accounts

exchange_b.name: Hyperliquid
strengths: Fully on-chain order book with sub-second finality
No KYC required with direct wallet connection
weaknesses: Single L1 ecosystem limits cross-chain arbitrage
Newer platform with limited operational history

rate_mechanics: Bybit uses standard 8-hour funding intervals with rates calculated from the premium/discount to spot prices across multiple exchanges. Hyperliquid implements 1-hour funding cycles, allowing for more frequent rate adjustments and shorter arbitrage windows. Both exchanges use similar funding rate formulas based on the perpetual-spot basis, but Hyperliquid's shorter intervals can lead to more volatile funding rates during high volatility periods.
verdict: Bybit offers proven infrastructure and broader market access with standard 8-hour funding cycles, making it suitable for traditional funding rate strategies. Hyperliquid's 1-hour funding intervals and on-chain transparency provide unique arbitrage opportunities but require faster execution and higher monitoring frequency.

faq.question: Which exchange has better funding rate opportunities?
faq.answer: Hyperliquid's 1-hour funding cycles create more frequent arbitrage windows, while Bybit's 8-hour intervals offer more predictable rate patterns. Choice depends on your strategy frequency and execution speed.

faq.question: How do trading fees compare for funding arbitrage?
faq.answer: Both exchanges charge 0.02% maker fees, but Bybit's 0.055% taker fee is slightly higher than Hyperliquid's 0.05%. For high-frequency funding strategies, this 0.005% difference impacts profitability.

faq.question: Which platform is better for institutional funding strategies?
faq.answer: Bybit offers proven institutional infrastructure with API stability and multiple settlement currencies. Hyperliquid provides transparency and faster funding cycles but has limited operational history.

faq.question: What are the settlement currency differences?
faq.answer: Bybit supports both USDT and USDC settlement with cross-collateral features. Hyperliquid only uses USDC, which may limit arbitrage opportunities but ensures consistent settlement mechanisms.

seo.title: Bybit vs Hyperliquid: Funding Rate Arbitrage Comparison
seo.description: Compare Bybit and Hyperliquid for perpetual futures funding rate arbitrage. Analyze 8h vs 1h funding cycles, fees, and settlement options for optimal strategies.
